observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to take the following
actions:
1. For the Concreting of Poblacion-Bulak Norte Road project:
1.a. Coordinate with the contractor to facilitate the submission and
processing of the final billing;
1.b. Issue the necessary certificates of project inspection, completion,
and acceptance; and
1.c. Reclassify the project into the appropriate asset account and
compute the corresponding depreciation for recording in the
books.
2. For the Construction of the Reinforced Concrete Drainage Canal:
2.a. Prepare a written report, supported by relevant documents,
detailing the circumstances of the alleged loss of materials due to
flooding.

2.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the
Municipal Accountant:
a. Determines the nature of the errors and implement the necessary
adjustments to correct the balance of the inter-agency payable and
other affected accounts; and
b. Recognizes the unrecorded assets amounting to ₱252,626.70 in the
books, including corresponding adjustments for the unrecorded
depreciation.
2.11 The Municipal Accountant during the exit conference acknowledged the
erroneous recordings and agreed to make the required adjustments.

3.14 We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Project
Management Committee (PMC):
a. Exerts all efforts to retrieve and submit to the Accounting Office the
list of program beneficiaries along with the corresponding valuation of
the supplies received; and
b. Expedites the issuance of demand letters to beneficiaries notifying
them of their payment obligations, and furnish copies to the
Accounting Office for reference.
3.15 We further recommended that the Management ensure the Municipal
Accountant records the receivables and closes the inventory account in the
books immediately upon receipt of the list of program beneficiaries from
the PMC.

4.9 We recommended that the Management ensure the Municipal Assessor:
a. Exhausts all efforts to gather the supporting documents necessary for
the titling of the lands owned by the LGU; and
b. Expedites the titling of all parcels of land owned by the LGU in
compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and furnish
the Municipal Accountant of the supporting documents for recording
purposes.
4.10 We also recommended that the Management ensure the Municipal
Accountant:
a. Makes the necessary adjustments or corrections for the unidentified
land recorded in the books, if warranted; and
b. Records in the books the land owned by the Municipality upon
receipt of the land titles, following Section 72 of the MNGAS for
LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund, the CY 2023 unutilized balances of the QRF and the DRRMF- MOOE in compliance with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the unexpended LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, after the lapse of five-year period, in compliance with Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect correct account balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in order to present reliable data in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Project Management Committee (PMC) to: a.1. Retrieve and submit to the Accounting Office the list of program beneficiaries with corresponding valuation of the supplies received; and a.2. Send demand letters to the beneficiaries informing them of their obligation to pay; and b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables and close the inventory account in the books, upon submission by the PMC of the list of program beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Determine the capital outlay component of CYs 2021 and 2022 unexpended LDRRMF included in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF; and b. Revert to the General Fund the unexpended DRRMF capital outlay included in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF, and this shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed, in accordance with Item 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and c. Prepare the necessary entries for the reversion of the funds to correct the overstatement of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the understatement of the Government Equity Account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Assessor’s Office to: a.1. Exhaust all efforts to gather the supporting documents necessary for the titling of the lands owned by the LGU; and a.2. Expedite the titling of all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the supporting documents for recording purposes. b. The Municipal Accountant to: b.1. Make the necessary adjustments or corrections for the unidentified land recorded in the books, if warranted; and b.2. Record in the books the land owned by the Municipality upon receipt of the land titles, following Section 72 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Convene the municipal officials/personnel responsible to formulate a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented PPA amounting to ₱18.189 million; and b. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in accordance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and c. Require the responsible officers to formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the programmed development projects within the required timeline.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Project Management Committee (PMC) to create an action plan to monitor the harvest time of each beneficiary, buy their produce and apply the proceeds to the loan balance and interest.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The concerned Implementing Offices to expedite the implementation of the projects under the fund transfers and submit the pertinent liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; and b. The Municipal Accountant to: b.1. Send confirmation letters to the SAs to ascertain the correct unexpended balance of fund transfer for projects that were already fully implemented by the LGU and facilitate the return of the excess funds to the SAs, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and b.2. Review the dormant unliquidated fund transfers and if warranted, request for authority to write-off, as provided for under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Instruct the Disposal Committee in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445, to determine the serviceability of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPEs) and facilitate the disposal process of the unserviceable properties; b. Direct the Disposal Committee to prepare the WMR and the IIRUP in consonance with Section 125 of NGAS Volume I; c. The General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to request in writing to the Office of the Auditor, for the disposal of unserviceable properties, upon submission of the IIRUP in accordance with Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992; and d. The Municipal Accountant, thereafter, to prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books of account all the disposed unserviceable properties to reflect the correct balance of the recorded PPE.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Provide additional manpower in the MDRRM Office to assist in the preparation of documents needed for the implementation of the PPAs, if warranted; and b. Direct the Responsible Officers to reassess all PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP that remained unimplemented to date and determine the necessity to implement the same otherwise, reprogram to priority PPAs and ensure its full implementation in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Direct the GSO to expedite the completion and submission to the Accounting Office of the supporting documents of the outstanding payables; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to: b.1. Ensure that all recorded payables have valid supporting documents on file in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445; and b.2. Verify the status of the recorded payables and make the necessary entries to record the cancellation, if warranted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to expedite the revision of the existing Revenue Code to increase its resources for a more efficient and effective implementation of its development plans, program objectives and priorities.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Ensure that funds were appropriated for the procurement of a vehicle compliant with the requirements set to transport residual wastes from the Municipality to the intended sanitary landfill; and b. Direct the responsible officers to implement the provisions of R.A. No. 9003, especially on the use of sanitary landfill or any other acceptable/authorized alternative types of dumpsite, follow up with the Department of Agrarian Reform (DAR) on the status of the access road opening and expedite the implementation of the safe closure plan of the dumpsite.

We recommended and the Management agreed to properly establish the LDRRMO, complete with the required staff, in accordance with Section 12 of Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, through the issuance of an appropriate Executive Order or Municipal Ordinance, stating their functions and responsibilities, to help ensure that the DRRM PPAs are properly implemented and monitored.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF, together with the RAAF, to the Office of the Auditor, not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month, in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Responsible Officer to: a. Submit monthly to the Auditor the Report on the Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in accordance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and b. Furnish the OCD and the DILG-OO a copy of the LDRRMFIP and of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, in compliance with Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a.(cid:9)Designate an official/employee to: a.1. Consolidate the PPA data from each department, and at the beginning of the year, provide the Office of the Auditor of the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year, consistent with Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and a.2. Prepare and submit the quarterly Report on Government PPA, including all of the ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA, to the Audit Team; and b. Direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA and submit it to the designated official/employee for consolidation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the GAD Focal Point Person to: a. Prioritize the inclusion of capacity development programs in the GPB, to include gender analysis and gender-responsive planning and budgeting, to capacitate the gender personnel involved in the preparation of GAD budget; b. Ensure that PPAs in GPB emanates from the results of current gender analysis; and c. Properly monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs to identify those that were not implemented in order that appropriate action is taken thereon.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: b. The Municipal Accountant to establish the RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred Income accounts at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers and amount collectible submitted by the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume I for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in order to present reliable data in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: 

The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year; and

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the General Services Officer to facilitate the preparation of the Property Cards, in accordance with Section 119, Chapter 7 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual;

Conduct the one-time cleansing of the PPE to establish the correct value of the account.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and submit the RPCPPE and every year thereafter;
The Municipal Accountant and GSO maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and reconcile their property records; and
The Municipality avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify reconciling items and provide the latter the needed data to effect adjustments on cash balance; and
The Offices concerned communicate with all the payees with long outstanding checks regarding the status of the checks issued and immediately take necessary action after the payee has returned the stale check to the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the concerned offices to submit the actual status of implementation of the program, projects and activities and consumption of the inventories to the accounting office in order to properly adjust the balance of the Inventory Account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to give priority on the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs in order to be prepared in case of disaster and to avoid possible loss of life and property.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRMF to the Auditor within the reglementary period using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the persons concerned to immediately look into the unimplemented development projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of non-implementation and formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the projects and prioritize what should be accomplished for its timely use without sacrificing the quality of work.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the General Services Officer or responsible personnel to submit, within five working days from execution/issuance, the Purchase Order and all the supporting documents for review of the Auditor.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality optimize the implementation of its existing revenue ordinances or update and create new ordinances, if warranted, to increase its resources for a more efficient and effective implementation of its development plans, program objectives and priorities.
We also recommended and the management agreed that the Municipality allocate funds for additional manpower and collection campaign/activities at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to increase collection of taxes and fees and intensify collection.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of the balances between the cash book and GL.

We recommended that the management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and prepare and submit the corresponding inventory reports; and

observations.reason: Due to BIR amounting to ₱675,690.40 refers to amount withheld in December 2016 and to be remitted in January 2017.

observations.recommendation: 14.1 We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the PS limitation provided in Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and Item 9.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 in the grant of PEI.
14.2 | We further recommended that the PEI granted to each of the officers and employees of the LGU-Batad be refunded.
observations.reason: Refund of PEI is already made thru deductions from the employees monthly salary and retirement claims.

observations.recommendation: 15.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures.
observations.reason: Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished in part but the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Reports of Official Travels were not accomplished and submitted.

observations.recommendation: 16.1 We recommended that the splitting of requisitions and purchase orders be avoided.
16.3 | Determine persons responsible and if warranted, institute proper disciplinary action to avoid incurrence of the same in the future, otherwise, the transaction will be disallowed in post-audit.
observations.reason: Compliance of requirements on the specific transaction referred to in the 2011 AAR is not ascertained.

observations.recommendation: 17.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive refrain from incurring additional PS cost until such time that the cap is observed.
observations.reason: Management abolished the GSO item and lumped its responsibility to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: 17.2 It is also advised that the Sangguniang Bayan adhere strictly with Section 5.3.3 of LBC No. 75 and Section 325(a) of RA 7160 in order to avoid disallowance of claims.
observations.reason: The Municipality is in compliance with PS Limitation for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: 18.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive avail of the services of the Appraisal Committee to appraise its properties so the Municipal Accountant could record them in the books of the LGU.
observations.reason: On-going process of titling

observations.recommendation: 18.2 We also recommended that the titling of the lots be ensured by inclusion of an appropriation in the budget for the purpose. Personnel should be assigned to work on the immediate titling of the parcels of land to establish ownership.
